This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

The CC-OFDM MIMO radar waveform designed herein is based on the requirement of surveillance radars used in coastal areas, where the target of interest are ships and small aircrafts in the area within 180 km from the radar site. OFDM signals with subcarrier spacing of 10 KHz will give a maximum unambiguous range of 15 km. source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

One-stage detectors, such as YOLO and SSD, excel in speed and computational efficiency, making them ideal for real-time applications. source_paragraph: align with the anomaly severity context, where the distance between true and predicted labels matters. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. source_paragraph: on critical findings. The proposed embedding, DroneSBERT, improved the trustworthiness of the model, as evidenced by the word’s importance report. Despite the promising results, the model’s generality remains untested as the training dataset contains only log messages acquired from 82 drone models, mostly from DJI. This is one of the main challenges in drone forensics research, where human-readable flight log datasets from other drone models are unavailable. In the future, we plan to explore the flight logs of other drone models to train more general anomaly detection models. As for the method, multi- objective learning that employs multiple loss functions to train an anomaly detection model will be explored. Instead of relying on resource-intensive transfer learning models, our approach optimizes model efficiency, making it more com- putationally friendly or resource-constrained applications. This contribution aims to streamline the implementation of emotion recognition systems without compromising perfor- mance. The proposed research is based on a simplified deep learn- ing architecture and channel selection approach that leads towards closer and resource-constrained real-time implemen- tation. In addition, research for cross-subject validation is more likely to be implemented widely than dependent subject validation. Cross-subject validation has a better generaliza- tion model than dependent-subject validation. source_paragraph: can be fine-tuned into our dataset. V. CONCLUSION V. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
